hooks.name: IAM Security Guardrail
hooks.description: Before writing to any CDK TypeScript stack or bin file (cdk/lib/*.ts or cdk/bin/*.ts), checks that the change complies with the IAM security policy rules. Skips all other file types silently.

action.prompt:
Check the path of the file being written. If it has a .md extension OR is NOT a CDK TypeScript file under cdk/lib/ or cdk/bin/ with a .ts extension, respond only with "Not a CDK stack file — skipping IAM check." and stop immediately.

Only if the file IS under cdk/lib/ or cdk/bin/ and has a .ts extension, verify the planned change does not violate any of these rules:
1. No wildcard Secrets Manager ARNs (resources: ['*'] or resources: ['arn:aws:secretsmanager:*:*:secret:*'])
2. No wildcard CloudWatch Logs ARNs (resources: ['arn:aws:logs:*:*:*'])
3. No banned managed policies: AmazonS3FullAccess or AmazonSSMReadOnlyAccess
4. No shared Lambda execution roles — each function group must have its own scoped role
5. No dangerous IAM actions: iam:AddUserToGroup or iam:*
6. RDS proxy connect must not use resources: ['*']
7. SSM parameter access must be scoped to the specific parameter path

If a rule is violated, explain which one and suggest the correct alternative. If compliant, confirm it is safe to proceed.
